zh.lifeSummary: 當William Mawde or Hartley於大約1513年出生在英國越可爾哈利法可時，他的父親John Arthur Mawde26歲，母親Margaret Bailey26歲。他於大約1534年與Maude Oldfield在英國英格蘭越可爾哈利法可結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世在英國英格蘭越可爾哈利法可，埋葬在英國越可爾哈利法可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Mawde or Hartley于大约1513年出生在英国越可爾哈利法可时，他的父亲John Arthur Mawde26岁，母亲Margaret Bailey26岁。他于大约1534年与Maude Oldfield在英国英格兰越可爾哈利法可结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世在英国英格兰越可爾哈利法可，埋葬在英国越可爾哈利法可。

nameMeanings.name: Mawde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin):

habitational name from Mold in Flintshire, Wales, which name derives from Anglo-Norman French mont haut ‘high hill’ (Latinised as mons altus), the Norman name for Bailey Hill on which the castle stood. The name de Montalt, de Mohaut was borne by an Anglo-Norman aristocratic family who acted as castle wardens there but who had their chief seat in Riddlesden in Yorkshire, where the surname proliferated as Mawhood and Maude. In the 17th century one branch of the family acquired lands in Ireland. The Maude baronetcy of Dundrum, Tipperary, was created in 1705 for Robert Maude. His son Cornwallis Maude, Baron de Montalt, was created first Viscount Hawarden in 1793. Mowatt is the Scottish form of the name, from a different branch of the same family.

occasionally from Middle English and Norman French female personal name Mahald or Maud, which are Old French forms of ancient Germanic Mahthild (Latinised as Matilda), composed of the Germanic elements maht ‘might, power’ + hild ‘battle’. This personal name partly owed its great popularity among Normans in England to the fact that it was the name of the wife (c. 1031–83) of William the Conqueror. Surnames derived from this personal name are found in many forms and spellings, for example, Mould and Moll .

variants.name: Mall
meanings: English: from the medieval female personal name Mall, a common form of Mald or Maud. See also Maude and Moll . It had several pet forms; see Malin , Malkin , and Mallet .
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit malla ‘strongman, wrestler’.

variants.name: Till
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Till, a pet form of Old French Matild(e) (in its Latin form Matilda), which was an alternative to Maud; see Maude 2. A diminutive form occurs in Tillotson .
North German: variant of   Thiel  .

variants.name: Mault
meanings: English: variant of   Maude   2.

variants.name: Mowatt
meanings: Scottish (northeastern): habitational name from Mold (Flintshire), see Maude 1. The distinctive Scottish form of the name has arisen from a shift of stress in Mohaut from the second to the first syllable. In Caithness and Orkney, the surname is traditionally pronounced ‘Mode’.

variants.name: Mould
meanings: English:
from the Middle English female personal names Mold, Moud, or Molt, shortened forms of Anglo-Norman French Mahold, a common variant of Mahald; see Maude 2.
habitational name from Mold (Flintshire, Wales).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Hartley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name, in northern England mainly from Hartley in Rochdale parish (Lancashire) but also from any of the places called Hartley in Westmorland and the West Yorkshire. In southern England it derives Hartley in Devon, Hampshire, and Kent, and from Hartleigh in Devon. Similar placenames occur in Berkshire, Dorset, and Northumberland, but it is not known if they gave rise to surnames. Most of the placenames derive from Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’, though the Westmorland placename comes from Old English heard ‘hard’ + clā ‘claw, tongue of land’, and the Northumberland placename derives from Old English heorot + hlāw ‘mound, hill’.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArtghaile ‘descendant of Artghal’, a personal name composed of the elements Art ‘bear’ or ‘hero’ + gal ‘valor’.

variants.name: Artley
meanings: English: variant of Hartley with loss of initial H-.

variants.name: Hartless
meanings: English (Staffordshire): variant of Hartley or of Hartle , with post-medieval excrescent -s. The spelling of the modern surname is no doubt due to confusion with the English vocabulary word heartless, meaning ‘cruel or unfeeling’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When William Mawde or Hartley was born about 1513, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, his father, John Arthur Mawde, was 26 and his mother, Margaret Bailey, was 26. He married Maude Oldfield about 1534, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 6 daughters. He died in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Halifax, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Mawde or Hartley est né environ 1513, à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Arthur Mawde, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret Bailey, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Maude Oldfield environ 1534, à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Mawde or Hartley um 1513 in Halifax, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Arthur Mawde 26 und seine Mutter Margaret Bailey 26. Er hat um 1534 in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Maude Oldfield geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Halifax, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Mawde or Hartley è nato il circa 1513 nel Halifax, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, John Arthur Mawde, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Bailey, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Maude Oldfield circa 1534, nel Halifax, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel Halifax, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Halifax, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Mawde or Hartleyが約1513年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスで生まれた時，父John Arthur Mawdeは26歳，母Margaret Baileyは26歳であった。約1534年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスでMaude Oldfieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスで死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Mawde or Hartley(이)가 대략 1513년 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Arthur Mawde(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Bailey(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1534년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 할이파스에서 Maude Oldfield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 할이파스에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Mawde or Hartley nasceu aproximadamente 1513, em Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Arthur Mawde, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Bailey, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Maude Oldfield aproximadamente 1534, em Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Mawde or Hartley родился около 1513 г. в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Arthur Mawde было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Bailey, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Maude Oldfield около 1534 г. в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Mawde or Hartley nació aproximadamente 1513, en Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Arthur Mawde, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Margaret Bailey, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Maude Oldfield aproximadamente 1534, en Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió en Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
